Requirement Description: The ICA agent (Buddy) must authenticate users via IBMid/SSO only. No anonymous access is permitted to the agent or the GitHub repository it connects to.
The GitHub Integration Suite tool connected to the agent must operate in read-only mode. No write or administrative operations are permitted through the agent.
API keys used to authenticate Bob Shell in CI/CD pipelines must be stored as encrypted GitHub Actions secrets and never hardcoded in source code or workflow files.
The agent must never reproduce or display secrets, API keys, passwords, tokens, or credentials found in the repository code or commit history.
No real customer data or production data must be accessible or surfaced through the agent even if present in logs or test files.
Storing a full JPA entity object in HttpSession must be flagged as a data serialization and exposure risk. Session should store minimal identifiers only.
The agent instructions must explicitly prevent prompt injection attacks. User inputs must not be able to override agent behavior or alter tool usage scope.
The agent must not execute code or run commands based on user input. All tool usage must be constrained to read operations defined in the system prompt.
The agent must never hallucinate technical information not present in the repository. If information is missing it must say so explicitly rather than generating unverified content.

Passwords stored in plain text in AdminServices and UserServices must be flagged as a critical security finding. BCryptPasswordEncoder is declared but not applied and this gap must be surfaced to developers.
CSRF protection is globally disabled in SecurityConfig. This must be flagged as a high-severity security gap in every automated audit report.
Hardcoded admin credentials seeded by DataLoader on startup must be flagged and documented as a known security risk requiring remediation before any production deployment.
The CI/CD pipeline must include a dependency vulnerability scan such as OWASP Dependency-Check to detect CVEs in Maven dependencies before merging to main.
Bob Shell installed via curl in CI must include integrity verification such as SHA-256 checksum to protect against supply chain attacks on the install script.
Chromium installed in the CI runner for PDF generation must use a pinned version to prevent version drift and unvetted updates introducing vulnerabilities.
Every CI workflow run must produce a traceable audit summary including trigger type, commit SHA, author identity, and list of changed files.
Agent interactions in ICA must be logged at the platform level. No sensitive data must appear in GitHub Actions step summaries or workflow logs.
The automated audit skill must run on every push to main and report all critical and high security findings with remediation priorities.
The Bob Shell API key must be scoped to Inference operations only and subject to a defined rotation policy with a maximum validity of 90 days.
Google OAuth2 credentials in application.properties must remain as placeholders in all committed code. Real credentials must only be injected at runtime via environment variables.
